title: What grace, O Lord, and beauty shone (revised)
firstLine: What grace, O Lord, and beauty shone
publicDomainBasis: Edward Denny (1796–1889), Samuel Arnold (1740–1802). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
What grace, O Lord, and beauty shone
Around Thy steps below!
What patient love was seen in all
Thy life and death of woe!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Thou love steadfast didst manifest
Through toilsome path extreme;
By pain, e’en death, wast not distressed—
Long-suffering love supreme!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Forever on Thy burdened heart
A weight of sorrow hung,
Yet no ungentle, murm’ring word
Escaped Thy silent tongue.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Thy foes did hate, despise, revile,
Thy friends unfaithful prove;
Unwearied in forgiveness still,
Thy heart could only love!

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
In all Thy living, fragrant, sweet,
Meal offering’s realized:
Fine, tender, balanced, and complete—
By God and man e’er prized.
